summary.intent: Address a third round of Codex review feedback on PR #673 for the ars-ui repo's Tour component, fix the identified bugs, and get the PR through CI and review to a mergeable state.
summary.outcome: Three new Codex review threads (spotlight padding/positioning offset coupling, controlled close ordering, AddStep step-change emission) were addressed with failing tests written first, then fixed. The commit was pushed and a fresh Codex review pass was triggered; CI went green on all jobs except release verification and coverage which were still running at session end.

repo: The Tour component's spotlight padding was incorrectly coupled to the positioning offset, causing visual-only changes to affect anchor calculations.
Controlled close must be sequenced before sync-geometry to avoid a stale geometry frame during dismissal.
AddStep operations that shift step indices must emit a StepChange event to keep consumers in sync.
The ars-ui repo uses a Codex bot reviewer triggered via @codex review comment; it signals active review with 👀 and completion with 👍 or unresolved threads.
PR labels like 'snapshot-reviewed' are managed via gh pr edit --add-label.
Co-author trailers are required on all commits; they were retrofitted via git filter-branch and a force-with-lease push.

code.finding: Tour state machine contains the spotlight positioning logic, step-change emission on AddStep, and controlled-close sequencing — all three were the locus of the third-pass fixes.
PositioningOptions and Offset structs define main_axis and related fields used by the Tour overlay; spotlight padding must not feed into these offset calculations.

workflow: Write failing tests first before patching behavior — the session explicitly followed a TDD loop for all three Codex findings.
cargo clippy cannot accept a test-filter argument the way cargo test can; passing one causes a malformed command that must be rerun without the filter.
Polling Codex review state requires two separate gh API calls: one for reactions (👀/👍) and one GraphQL query counting unresolved Codex-authored threads.
A long active 👀 period with no threads is ambiguous — it may be a slow review or a stall; treat it as active until the reaction drops.
Unrelated formatter diffs (e.g., dioxus fmt changes) should be stashed before committing to keep commits scoped to the reviewed changes.

friction: The third Codex review pass took an unusually long time (~30+ minutes of active 👀) before producing threads, requiring many redundant poll cycles.
git filter-branch required FILTER_BRANCH_SQUELCH_WARNING=1 and a force-with-lease push to retrofit co-author trailers, adding ceremony to a routine fixup.
cargo clippy accepted a spurious test-filter argument without erroring cleanly, producing a malformed invocation that had to be diagnosed and rerun.
Resolving the five second-pass review threads required individual API calls in a for-loop rather than a batch operation, making the resolution verbose.
open_items: Release verification and coverage CI jobs were still in progress at session end — their final status was not confirmed.
The unrelated dioxus fmt diff in crates/ars-dioxus/tests/attr_passthrough.rs was stashed and not committed; it needs a separate commit or PR.
A fourth Codex review pass was triggered but its outcome (approval or new threads) was not observed in this session.
